docs.purpose: Tracks every development session on Sorriva. Used by the Velocity Dashboard to calculate development velocity, estimated cost savings vs traditional development, and cumulative AI-assisted hours. Also provides a historical record of what shipped when.
docs.blended_rate: Hourly rate in USD representing what a traditional developer would charge for equivalent work. Used to calculate cost savings vs AI-assisted development. Set to $170/hr — a blended rate for senior full-stack + design + product work in the US market.
docs.claude_monthly_cost: Monthly subscription cost of Claude in USD. Used to calculate ROI: (estimated_hours * blended_rate) / claude_monthly_cost.
docs.duration_hrs: Equivalent traditional developer hours for the session — NOT the user's actual time or wall-clock time. Represents how long a senior human developer (or team) would have taken to design, build, test, and ship the same scope of work. Calculated by summing the complexity-hour values of all features shipped: XS=3hrs, S=8hrs, M=20hrs, L=40hrs, XL=80hrs. For planning/architecture sessions with no features shipped, duration_hrs is set manually based on equivalent analyst/architect/product time for the scope of work produced.
docs.estimated: Boolean. true = duration_hrs was computed from complexity ratings of features_shipped or manually estimated for planning sessions. false = duration_hrs was set manually with high confidence. owner_hrs is never estimated — always user-reported.
docs.features_shipped: Array of roadmap feature IDs (e.g. fLibraryScanner) shipped in this session. Cross-references roadmap-data.json for complexity, phase, and description.
docs.version: The app version at the end of this session. Matches version.json.
session_close_checklist: 1. Mark completed roadmap features as done=true in roadmap-data.json
2. PROMPT USER: 'How many hours did you personally spend on this session?' Record answer as owner_hrs. Do not estimate — must come from the user.

3. Append session entry to sessions.json: session_id (YYYY-MM-DD-N), date, duration_hrs (sum of complexity hours for features shipped, or manual estimate for planning sessions), owner_hrs (user-reported), estimated, version, features_shipped, notes
4. Update sorriva-handoff.html — bump version, update current state, next priorities
5. Build final deploy zip: app.py (if changed), frontend HTML, handoff, roadmap-data.json, sessions.json
docs.velocity_dashboard_fields: The Velocity Dashboard will compute: total_duration_hrs (sum of all duration_hrs — equivalent traditional dev hours), total_owner_hrs (sum of all owner_hrs — user's actual time invested), estimated_cost_savings (total_duration_hrs * blended_rate), roi_multiple (estimated_cost_savings / cumulative_claude_cost), avg_duration_hrs_per_session, avg_owner_hrs_per_session, leverage_ratio (duration_hrs / owner_hrs per session — how many equiv dev hours per hour of user time), features_per_session, and a timeline chart of cumulative equivalent hours and savings by version.
docs.owner_hrs: Tom's actual hours spent in the session — reported by the user at session close. Never estimated by Claude. Represents time as product owner, solutions architect, QA, and domain expert. Null if the user did not report before session closed. At session close, Claude must ask: 'How many hours did you personally spend on this session?' and record the answer here before finalizing.
docs.session_id: Unique identifier for each session. Format: YYYY-MM-DD-N where N is a 1-based sequence number for sessions on the same date (e.g. '2026-06-24-1', '2026-06-24-2'). Always increment N for each new session on the same day — check existing entries before appending to avoid collision.

notes: Session durations are estimated based on scope of work, features shipped, and complexity. Not wall-clock time — represents active development time equivalent. Sorriva is a pure App Store product — iOS/iPadOS primary, Apple TV Phase 6. No self-hosted server for app logic.

sessions.notes: Project inception and full product definition session — no code shipped. Defined complete feature registry across 8 categories: local library, blended library, metadata enrichment, Billboard archive, favorites, streaming sources (Spotify, Qobuz, iHeartRadio, SiriusXM, iTunes/Apple Music), zones and audio output (Sonos, Bluesound, defined/adhoc groups, remote streaming, AirPlay, CarPlay), AI playlists and discovery (Deriva engine, mood-based generation, skip learning, library rediscovery), playback stats, play queue, auto play, Shazam integration, offline playback, iOS widgets, Bluesound Vault support, server management. Defined full tech stack: Flask/Python backend, SQLite, FFmpeg, React Native Expo Bare, Cloudflare Tunnel, reused Tack deployment pipeline. Architecture principles locked: zero hardcoded paths, env vars throughout, multi-instance ready, clean API separation (/api/v1/), service layer pattern, WebSocket for real-time, auth middleware stub, commercial-ready schema. 8-phase development plan defined. UI mockups completed for Library, Now Playing, Artist, Zones, Apple TV screens — warm parchment theme, slate blue accent, brass secondary, SVG icon system. Named the product: Sorriva (sonic + deriva — the sound that drifts). Domain sorriva.app registered ($14.20/yr, Cloudflare). Wordmark locked: Inter 800, -0.05em tracking, slate blue dot — same DNA as Tack. Primary tagline: Your Music. Your Way. Secondary: Bring the vinyl experience to your digital collection. AI discovery engine named Deriva. duration_hrs set manually: equivalent senior product/architect/designer time to define a product of this scope from scratch.

Major architecture pivot session — no code shipped. Full pivot from self-hosted Flask/Python server on Mac Mini to pure App Store product. Key decisions: self-hosted server eliminated entirely; AirPlay 2 as multi-room backbone (all of Tom's hardware confirmed compatible: Era 100, Era 300, Roam, Ultra Arc, Bluesound Node); AirPlay 2 ecosystem confirmed audiophile-grade (B&O, Devialet, Naim, KEF, Marantz, Denon, Yamaha, NAD); unified music graph concept locked (one track identity, MusicBrainz MBID, source resolved at playback time); Spotify Connect + MusicKit as in-app playback path (no app switching); Android excluded — Apple-first is deliberate; Mac app as optional library bridge; external USB-C drive support for iPad (iPadOS 13+); offline owned music = full Sorriva download, streaming offline = accepted limitation; EQService abstraction layer (one UI, per-device API translation); hardware freedom as key marketing differentiator. music-app-context.md fully rewritten to reflect new architecture. Marketing positioning section added: target user profile, competitive framing table, candidate taglines, hardware freedom differentiator. Branded HTML context doc (sorriva-context.html) produced in Sorriva brand system. Sorriva marketing splash page (sorriva-index.html) produced with letter-by-letter wordmark reveal animation, ambient ring expansion, scroll-triggered pillar reveals, four product value pillars with inline illustrations, and email capture CTA. duration_hrs set manually: equivalent senior product strategist + architect + designer + front-end developer time for scope of decisions made and deliverables produced.

Foundation session. GitHub repo created at github.com/fournit/sorriva and scaffold pushed (fGitHubRepo). Expo Bare project initialized at ~/projects/sorriva-app with SDK 57, iOS/iPadOS target, bundle ID app.sorriva.ios, supportsTablet: true, Xcode workspace generated via prebuild (fXcodeProject). Sessions.json and roadmap-data.json reconciled and marked done (fSessionsJson). Roadmap significantly revised: fCloudBackend rescoped to fMinimalBackend (hub health only — no app server logic), fUserAccounts rescoped to household + member profile model with Apple Sign-In and per-profile streaming OAuth credentials (Roon household model locked as commercial strategy), fHouseholdProfiles merged into fUserAccounts, fLocalSQLiteCache renamed fAppDataSchema, fNavShell added as new Phase 1 feature (tab bar + screen stubs), tvOS deferred to Phase 6. Architecture decision locked: subscription unit is household, members have individual streaming credentials, commercial pricing follows Roon model. Design mockups produced: Apple TV Now Playing (6 tabs: Playing/Art/Info/Credits/Review/Artist, light/dark modes, EQ bars), iPhone library/Deriva/Now Playing screens — saved to design-reference.md.
